title: The knife-sharpener who walked our street
summary: Tell the story of an encounter with a travelling knife-sharpener.
topic: An encounter with a travelling knife-sharpener
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences telling the story of meeting someone who does an unusual or old-fashioned job. Say what the person did, how you met them, and what happened during the meeting. Use past simple.
hintStarters: One morning last summer, I heard a strange sound from the street below…
The man stopped his bicycle outside our door and…
My grandmother went downstairs immediately because…

hintVocab.meaningVi: bị thu hút, bị mê hoặc

sampleText: One summer morning, I heard a strange high sound coming from the street below our apartment in Seville. I looked out of the window and saw a man on a bicycle with a small grinding wheel mounted above the back wheel. He was pedalling slowly and blowing a tin whistle. My grandmother heard the sound from the kitchen. She immediately picked up two kitchen knives from the drawer and told me to come with her. We went downstairs. The man stopped his bicycle when he saw her. He did not say anything. She handed him the knives. He pressed each blade against the spinning stone and a few sparks flew out into the morning air. I stood very close and watched. The sound was loud and metallic. The whole job took less than five minutes. When he finished, he tested the edge of each knife against his thumbnail and gave them back. My grandmother paid him two euros and thanked him. He nodded, put the whistle back in his shirt pocket, and rode slowly up the street. His whistle faded around the corner. My grandmother said he came to our street every few months. She never remembered his name. She said it did not matter — she just knew the sound.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một bánh mài nhỏ gắn phía trên bánh sau

sampleAnnotations.phrase: pedalling slowly and blowing a tin whistle
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đạp xe chậm rãi và thổi chiếc còi thiếc

sampleAnnotations.phrase: immediately picked up two kitchen knives from the drawer
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ngay lập tức lấy hai con dao bếp từ ngăn kéo

sampleAnnotations.phrase: He pressed each blade against the spinning stone
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Ông ấy ép từng lưỡi dao vào đá mài đang quay

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a few sparks flew out into the morning air
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: vài tia lửa bắn ra không khí buổi sáng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The sound was loud and metallic
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Âm thanh nghe to và sắc như kim loại

sampleAnnotations.phrase: tested the edge of each knife against his thumbnail
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: thử độ sắc của từng lưỡi dao bằng móng tay cái

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: gật đầu, cất còi trở lại túi áo sơ mi
đạp xe chậm lên phía đầu phố

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tiếng còi của ông mờ dần quanh góc phố
Bà không bao giờ nhớ tên ông ấy
bà chỉ nhận ra âm thanh đó

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for a short sequence of actions
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong một câu chuyện ngắn, dùng past simple để liệt kê các hành động theo thứ tự. Lưu ý chuỗi: 'heard → looked → saw → went → stopped → handed → pressed → flew → stood → watched → tested → gave → paid → thanked → nodded → rode → faded'. Mỗi động từ là một bước tiếp theo.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: In a short story, use past simple to list actions in order. Notice the chain: 'heard → looked → saw → went → stopped → handed → pressed → flew → stood → watched → tested → gave → paid → thanked → nodded → rode → faded'. Each verb is the next step.

sampleTranslationVi: Một buổi sáng mùa hè, tôi nghe thấy một âm thanh lạ cao vang lên từ con phố bên dưới căn hộ của chúng tôi ở Seville. Tôi nhìn qua cửa sổ và thấy một người đàn ông đi xe đạp với một bánh mài nhỏ gắn phía trên bánh sau. Ông đạp xe chậm rãi và thổi chiếc còi thiếc. Bà tôi nghe thấy âm thanh đó từ bếp. Bà ngay lập tức lấy hai con dao bếp từ ngăn kéo và bảo tôi đi cùng. Chúng tôi xuống lầu. Người đàn ông dừng xe khi thấy bà. Ông không nói gì. Bà đưa cho ông những con dao. Ông ép từng lưỡi dao vào đá mài đang quay và vài tia lửa bắn ra không khí buổi sáng. Tôi đứng rất gần và nhìn. Âm thanh nghe to và sắc như kim loại. Toàn bộ công việc mất chưa đến năm phút. Khi xong, ông thử độ sắc của từng lưỡi dao bằng móng tay cái và trả lại. Bà tôi trả cho ông hai euro và cảm ơn. Ông gật đầu, cất còi trở lại túi áo sơ mi và đạp xe chậm lên phía đầu phố. Tiếng còi của ông mờ dần quanh góc phố. Bà tôi nói ông đến phố của chúng tôi vài tháng một lần. Bà không bao giờ nhớ tên ông. Bà nói điều đó không quan trọng — bà chỉ nhận ra âm thanh đó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best opening for a story about something you heard outside.
options: One summer morning, I heard a strange sound from the street below.
One summer morning, I hear a strange sound from the street below.
One summer morning, I was hear a strange sound from the street below.
One summer morning, I have heard a strange sound from the street below.
mcQuestions.explanation: A completed past event uses past simple: 'heard'. 'Hear' is present; 'was hear' is wrong; 'have heard' is present perfect, not used for a specific past morning.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense describes a finished action on a specific morning in the past?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes what the man was doing as he moved down the street?
options: He was pedalling slowly and blowing a tin whistle.
He pedalled slowly and blows a tin whistle.
He was pedalling slowly and was blew a tin whistle.
He pedals slowly and blows a tin whistle.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous ('was pedalling', 'blowing') describes ongoing background actions when the story starts. Both verbs need the same tense; 'blew' is past simple, not past continuous.
mcQuestions.hint: What tense is used for two ongoing actions happening at the same time in the background?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past simple of 'give'.
options: He gave the knives back.
He gived the knives back.
He given the knives back.
He gives the knives back.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Gave' is the irregular past simple of 'give'. 'Gived' is wrong; 'given' is the past participle; 'gives' is present simple.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the irregular past simple of 'give'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'immediately' in the correct position?
options: She immediately picked up the knives.
She picked immediately up the knives.
Immediately she picks up the knives.
She picked up immediately the knives.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Immediately' modifies the verb and goes before the main verb (after the subject). Placing it between the verb and its object or particle is not natural.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does a time adverb like 'immediately' go in relation to the main verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means metal shining pieces that fly when a blade is sharpened?
options: blades
sparks
edges
pedals
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Sparks' are the small bright fragments that fly when metal is ground or struck. 'Blades' are knife edges; 'edges' are borders; 'pedals' are foot levers.
mcQuestions.hint: What flies through the air when metal touches a spinning grindstone?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that correctly uses 'told someone to do something'.
options: She told me to come with her.
She told me come with her.
She told to me to come with her.
She said me to come with her.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Tell someone to do something' = 'told + object + to + infinitive'. 'Tell to someone' is wrong; 'say someone' is wrong.
mcQuestions.hint: What structure follows 'tell' when giving an instruction to a person?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past simple of 'ride'.
options: He rode slowly up the street.
He rided slowly up the street.
He riden slowly up the street.
He rides slowly up the street.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Rode' is the irregular past simple of 'ride'. 'Rided' is wrong; 'riden' is not a standard form; 'rides' is present simple.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the irregular past simple of 'ride'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes the sound getting quieter and disappearing?
options: His whistle faded around the corner.
His whistle disappeared around the corner.
His whistle faded in the corner.
His whistle faded at the corner.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Faded around the corner' means the sound became gradually quieter as the source moved away. 'Around the corner' means the sound continued past a bend in the road. 'In the corner' and 'at the corner' describe a fixed point.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase means the sound gradually became quieter as the source went past a turn?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct frequency phrase for something that happens several times a year.
options: every few months
every few month
each few months
every some months
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Every few months' is the standard phrase for an irregular but recurring event several times a year. 'Month' must be plural; 'each few' and 'every some' are not standard.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase uses 'every' + 'few' + a plural noun to express irregular frequency?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says someone never remembered something?
options: She never remembered his name.
She not remembered his name.
She didn't never remember his name.
She never remembered his names.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Never' with a positive past simple verb creates a negative meaning. Double negatives ('didn't never') are nonstandard. 'Name' is singular here (one person, one name).
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'never' need a negative auxiliary? And how many names does one person have?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that best shows someone is fascinated by watching a skilled person work.
options: I stood very close and watched.
I stood very close and was watching.
I stood near and was fascinated watching.
I stand very close and I watch.
mcQuestions.explanation: Two simple past actions in sequence: 'stood and watched'. The simplicity mirrors the direct observation. Option B is also possible but adds unnecessary continuity.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option uses simple past to describe two actions in sequence most cleanly?

criticalThinkingQuestion: The grandmother never remembered the knife-sharpener's name but always recognised his sound. Is it strange to have a relationship with someone you know only by their sound or job? Can you think of similar people in your own life?
